title: Distrust toward artificial intelligence grows in the United States
summary: A survey shows that Americans have lost faith in AI, while figures like Meredith Whittaker warn about chatbots and singles reject technological dependency.

title: Polymarket accused of paying for misleading videos about fake bets
summary: The decentralized betting platform Polymarket faces accusations of having created misleading viral content to attract users.

title: Anthropic implements identity verification for certain Claude features
summary: Starting July 2026, Claude users will need to verify their identity to access certain capabilities.

title: Concern over the use of AI in school education grows
summary: Studies indicate that AI in schools may cause more harm than good, while Norway becomes the first country to ban it in primary school.

title: Apple expands Siri and AI capabilities in iOS 27
summary: The new beta version of iOS 27 includes practical artificial intelligence tools beyond Siri.

title: The high cost of memory forces product cancellations and price increases
summary: Nothing abandons new CMF smartphones and Apple signals price increases due to the rising cost of memory chips.

title: Brands turn to AI-generated influencers and personalized ads
summary: Companies like HBO Max implement AI-powered ads and more and more brands use virtual avatars to promote products on social media.

title: Worker morale in the US and at Meta hits record lows
summary: Meta executives admit they have crushed employee spirit, while a study reveals that the majority of American workers are disengaged.

title: AI transforms the job market and creates demand for new trades
summary: Nvidia anticipates a great need for electricians and plumbers, while artificial intelligence creates a two-speed job market.

title: China advances in autonomous technology and orbital data centers
summary: A report shows Chinese dominance in robotaxis, while the country prepares AI data centers in orbit to compete with SpaceX.

title: US develops anti-drone lasers and China publishes manual for attacking aircraft carriers
summary: The Pentagon is working on laser weapons for air defense, while China spreads tactics for hunting American aircraft carriers.

title: Valve and Beyond All Reason: new clues about Half-Life 3 and a free RTS
summary: Valve seems to hint at the release of Half-Life 3 through a variable in Dota 2, while the free game Beyond All Reason pays homage to Total Annihilation.

title: Microsoft improves search in Windows 11 and file management
summary: An unlocked version of Windows search removes Bing and speeds up the system, while the user interface evolves to handle unassociated files.

title: Software deals: Office 2024 license and Amazon discounts
summary: Platforms offer lifetime licenses of Office and discounts on Amazon devices before Prime Day.

title: Underwater innovations: from homemade submarines to fishing robots
summary: A Chinese farmer builds submarines in his spare time, while fish-shaped robots automate sashimi cutting.

title: AI as a threat to personal productivity
summary: Tim Ferriss warns that ChatGPT replaces his productivity advice, while Wired offers tips to improve ChatGPT prompts.

title: AI industry faces criticism and potential regulations
summary: Yann LeCun labels xAI a failure, while the impact of a potential regulatory crackdown on Anthropic is analyzed.

title: AI boosts the real estate market and forces cost cutting
summary: Tech companies limit the use of AI to reduce expenses, while artificial intelligence boosts luxury home sales in the US.

title: New tools for developers: mobile dashboard for Claude and MCP server
summary: Pulse offers a dashboard to manage Claude Code from mobile, while Cloudback launches an MCP server for backups.

title: New web search and discovery platforms
summary: In the Weights presents itself as an AI-centered search engine, while Notchkin offers a product discovery tool.
